response.justification: The candidate is an experienced, versatile senior engineer with strong systems-programming credentials (low-latency DLang services, async I/O, high-throughput microservices) and solid craftsmanship practices, but the role centers on hands-on modern C++ development for industrial automation. Her C++ experience is limited to a short stint at National Instruments (~6 months, 2014–2015) and a thesis project, and she shows no exposure to MQTT, message brokers in an industrial context, IEC 62443/SDL practices, or OT protocols. The recent career trajectory (TypeScript/Node full-stack, AI tooling) points away from the C++/industrial-cybersecurity focus this position requires.
strengths: Deep systems experience: low-latency, high-throughput services using async I/O, fibers, and caching (Dunnhumby/Sociomantic)
Strong engineering practices: TDD, CI/CD, pairing, testable design, plus mentoring and workshop/teaching track record
Broad full-stack and DevOps versatility (Node.js, TypeScript, Docker, Kubernetes, embedded/desktop exposure)
concerns: Minimal professional C++ experience (~6 months in 2014–2015); primary languages are TypeScript/Node and DLang
No evidence of industrial automation, IEC 62443/SDL, MQTT, or OT protocol experience (Modbus, OPC UA, etc.)
Recent focus on web product and AI-agent tooling suggests misalignment with a hands-on C++ industrial platform role

key_factors.factor: Lack of substantial C++/STL professional experience
No industrial cybersecurity (IEC 62443/SDL) or MQTT/OT protocol background
Proven high-performance networked systems and concurrency experience (DLang)
